input: Neural Network Architecture: Three layer types — Input Layer (1, each node = one feature), Hidden Layers (0 to many, add complexity and learn patterns), Output Layer (1, final prediction). Components: Nodes/Neurons hold values, Weights are learning parameters connecting nodes (adjusted during training), Bias added to weighted sum. Forward Propagation: Input → [Weighted Sum → Activation] → Hidden → Output. At each layer: weighted_sum = (inputs · weights_transpose) + bias, then output = Activation(weighted_sum). Activation Functions: ReLU (hidden layers, most popular, introduces nonlinearity), Sigmoid (output, binary classification), Softmax (output, multi-class). Without activation functions, multiple layers = just linear regression.
